title: verbascum × hybridum
intro: Verbascum × hybridum, also known as the common mullein, is a biennial plant that belongs to the Scrophulariaceae family. It has been used in herbal medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, skin irritations, and wounds, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The leaves and flowers can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties, and have also been used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
